hypothesis: Enabling the existing, currently-unused schema_enum_close_decode_weight decode bias on E620's frozen rejected checkpoint fixes Modal's optional enum-valued `size` argument garbage literal (E635's named next step: "target Modal's property-role mismatch using the same active-property compatibility principle, without changing the now-correct Auth path") without regressing Auth or Dashboard.

blocker.summary: outputs/ is not persisted across agent sessions (gitignored; scratch checkpoints are local-only under --no-sync-checkpoints per docs/design/checkpoint-bucket.md). E630-E635 all say "reused" E620's checkpoint, SHA-256 3ce5c9efc70ed69c7a6680129018ec0aa2061f56020ff42301faf144363ecc5f, but that file did not exist at the start of this session. Retraining the exact documented E620 recipe reproduces the source data lineage and a comparable loss, but not the checkpoint bytes or, more importantly, its qualitative greedy-decode behavior: an 800-step scratch TwoTower is barely converged, so tiny cross-environment floating-point differences (this session resolved torch==2.5.1 fresh via uv; the original run's exact build is unknown/unpinned) cascade through ~150+ greedy argmax decode steps into a different decode path.
blocker.action_not_taken: Did not run the schema_enum_close_decode_weight treatment against a non-representative substitute checkpoint, because neither reproduction attempt's OOD suite ever emits a literal Modal(...)/Input(...)/Button(...) call (see per_record_treatment below) -- the specific Modal `size`-argument mismatch this experiment targets cannot occur in either substitute checkpoint's decode path, so any comparison would be a fabricated positive/negative, not evidence.

decision: No treatment run against a substitute checkpoint. Document the reproducibility gap as blocking this session's continuation of the E630-E635 checkpoint-reuse thread. Recommend one of: (1) sync/persist a designated frozen checkpoint for this active multi-session ablation lineage instead of --no-sync-checkpoints, (2) pin exact torch/numpy build versions for byte-reproducible scratch trains, or (3) migrate the ablation to a fully-trained/promoted checkpoint immune to 800-step scratch instability. Neither new local checkpoint (v1, v2) is synced or promoted; both are discarded scratch artifacts.
